entries.context: WEAK_BUY signal from enhanced research, checklist score 100/100, 79% historical win rate on WEAK_BUY
entries.expectations: Price reaches $40 by Aug 10 earnings (7.6% gain). Thesis: GLP-1 market leader with accelerating revenue, telehealth moat.
entries.biases_checked: FOMO - no, entered on pullback from $38. Recency - no, based on multi-factor analysis.
entries.thesis: HIMS is a GLP-1 market leader with accelerating revenue growth and telehealth platform providing competitive moat. Q2 earnings on Aug 10 are the catalyst. WEAK_BUY signal has 79% historical win rate. Position sized at 10% based on conviction.
entries.why_wrong: 1) GLP-1 market saturates faster than expected 2) Competitors take market share 3) Earnings miss expectations 4) Market correction hits growth stocks

what_worked: Systematic stop loss execution - loss contained within parameters
Position sizing appropriate for conviction level
Pre-defined exit rules followed without emotion
what_didnt_work: Entry timing - bought into early strength without waiting for pullback confirmation
Over-reliance on checklist score without price action context
Did not account for broader market weakness on entry day
lessons_extracted.pattern_insight: WEAK_BUY + earnings catalyst needs price confirmation within 48 hours or early stop recommended
lessons_extracted.system_update: Add price momentum check: require close > prior day high OR wait for pullback to 20 SMA

outcome.lessons: Stop loss respected. Position sized correctly. System worked as designed. Loss contained within risk parameters.

entries.context: Checklist score 100/100, financial model shows +161% upside, AI infrastructure beneficiary
entries.expectations: Fair value $70.23, potential 3-6 month hold for AI infrastructure play
entries.emotional_state: Excited by valuation, cautious about timing
entries.biases_checked: Value trap? Check cyclical nature. Don't anchor to low P/E alone.
entries.thesis: SMCI is undervalued AI infrastructure play at 14.5x P/E vs 25-30x peers. Revenue growing 16% CAGR. Monthly business updates provide catalyst visibility. Strong technical setup.
entries.why_wrong: 1) AI capex cycle peaks sooner than expected 2) Competition from DELL/HPQ intensifies 3) Margin compression in commoditized server market 4) Accounting/quality concerns resurface

entries.context: User directive: You run the portfolio by yourself, you never ask me for execution
entries.expectations: Stop losses hit = automatic exit. Targets hit = automatic scale-out. Signals trigger = automatic entry within sizing limits.
entries.thesis: Autonomous operation removes emotional decision-making and enforces discipline. System executes based on predefined rules without human intervention.
entries.why_wrong: Edge cases not covered by rules, market conditions change requiring human judgment, technical failures
